review: This trug is everything I hoped for. The chestnut slats have a lovely warm color and the willow frame is surprisingly sturdy. I have been using it to carry cut hostas and echinacea from the garden and it handles everything gracefully. The linseed oil finish smells wonderful. It arrived beautifully packed. My second purchase from this shop and certainly not my last.

These wildflower notecards are simply beautiful. The letterpress impression is deep and satisfying to the touch — you can feel the pressure of each flower petal printed into the cotton paper. I sent them to a friend recovering from surgery and she wrote back to say the card alone lifted her spirits. I keep a set in my writing desk at all times now.

A perfect birthday gift for my spouse Harriet. The blue-grey glaze is exactly as shown — quiet and lovely, with that characteristic variation that only handmade pottery has. It arrived triple-padded and perfectly safe. Harriet uses it every morning for decaf tea and says it is the nicest mug they have ever owned. The craftsmanship is exceptional.

I ordered the monogrammed trug with the letter H for Harriet and it is absolutely perfect. The pyrography initial is clean and beautifully placed on the handle — not at all burned-looking but properly elegant. The trug itself is the same high quality as the one I ordered before. Harriet took it straight out to the vegetable patch. A five-star gift.

I ordered these pressed-flower notecards for my dear friend Peggy and she was quite moved by them. The echinacea petals are the most vivid coral-pink — clearly pressed at their peak. Each card is individual and you can feel the acetate window sitting over the actual flower. Peggy is a retired librarian and has an eye for quality; she immediately said these were the most beautiful cards she had ever received.

I asked for a bookmark for my grandson Oliver with a short verse from The Hobbit on it and the maker delivered something quite lovely. The watercolor botanical border in soft greens and golds is exactly right for a 13-year-old boy who reads fantasy — not too childish, properly handsome. Oliver was thrilled and immediately put it in whichever book he is reading. Wonderful work.

An exceptionally well-curated gift set. I ordered it for my daughter Claire with her bookplates personalized to her name. The pressed-flower bookmarks, the wildflower notecards, and the bookplates all arrived together in a lovely kraft box. It looked as if it came from a proper independent bookshop. Claire called me on Sunday to say it was her favourite gift of the year. Highly recommended.

This deep harvest trug is magnificent. I did not think I needed a larger trug after my first one but I was wrong. The ash slats are beautifully finished and the bentwood handle is strong and comfortable. I can carry an entire armload of black-eyed Susans without them tipping. It lives on the potting bench and gets used every single day in the growing season.

The hosta notecard set stopped me cold when I opened the envelope. The letterpress hosta leaf is printed in the most exact forest green with chartreuse veins — it is unmistakably a hosta and unmistakably done by someone who knows plants. I collect hosta cultivars and I have never seen a card that captured the plant so precisely. A beautiful thing.

I bought this framed pressed-flower piece for my granddaughter Bea's birthday. She is studying marine biology and loves the natural world in every form. The arrangement of black-eyed Susans and native ferns in the natural wood frame is quietly beautiful — it would suit a teenager's room without looking childish. Bea hung it above her desk. Perfect in every way.

The matched monogrammed trowel and fork set for Harriet is perfectly made. Both handles bear the same clean H initial and they are pleasingly balanced in the hand. The stainless heads feel solid and serious — not at all like the flimsy garden tools you find in hardware stores. Harriet uses both daily and has been showing them off to everyone at the Glastonbury Garden Club.

I ordered these bookplates for my grandson Oliver — 12 plates printed with his full name in a clean navy letterpress hand. They are exactly right: dignified but not stuffy, the kind of bookplate a serious young reader can be proud of. Oliver has been systematically working through his bookshelf putting them in. A small thing that clearly meant a great deal to him.

This gift basket for my daughter Claire and son-in-law Thomas was the most satisfying purchase I have made this year. The monogrammed trug, trowel, sage green mug, and notecards all arrived together in a proper basket with tissue. It looked curated and generous without being excessive. Claire rang me on Sunday to say Thomas nearly cried opening it. The quality throughout was exceptional.

My second order of the pressed-flower notecard set, this time as a Christmas gift for Peggy. She sends proper handwritten notes to everyone and these cards are worthy of the occasion. The cosmos and sweet alyssum in this batch were particularly beautiful — deep pink with the most delicate dried petals. I am now buying these for every notable gift occasion.

The sage green mug is lovely — the iron speckles in the glaze catch the light in a quiet, satisfying way. I ordered it for my own morning peppermint tea and it has become my favourite mug without question. The weight is right, the handle sits comfortably, and the 14 oz size is generous. Dishwasher safe as claimed: I have washed it dozens of times and the glaze is still perfect.

My third trug from this shop — this one for the herb garden by the back door. The quality is completely consistent across all three orders: tight joinery, smooth chestnut slats, a handle that will not crack. I have recommended this shop to every member of the Glastonbury Garden Club and three of them have ordered already. This is simply the best garden trug available anywhere.

Ordered a second reader's companion set, this time with bookplates personalized for my granddaughter Bea. She is a voracious reader and the set was exactly right for her. The pressed-flower bookmarks particularly suited her — she studies marine biology and has a love of the natural world in all its forms. The bookplates are properly printed: navy, clean, serious. Bea was delighted.

A beautifully made monogrammed trowel for Harriet to start the new growing season. The H initial is burned cleanly into the smooth hardwood handle with confident strokes. The balance is good and the stainless head is properly heavy. Harriet has an artisan's eye for craft and examined it very carefully before pronouncing it well made. I trust that judgement completely.

My fourth set of wildflower notecards. I keep them in the drawer of my writing desk and reach for them whenever I want to send a proper note rather than an email. The impression of the echinacea and black-eyed Susan is extraordinarily satisfying. They are now on a recurring order in my mind — whenever I run low I simply return here. Consistent quality, every time.

My latest monogrammed trug order — the H initial for Harriet again, this time for our anniversary. The maker knows this is a repeat order and the quality has not wavered in the slightest. The pyrography on the handle is exactly as clean as the first time. The chestnut wood always has a slightly different grain and color to it which I find lovely — each trug is genuinely individual. Outstanding.
